conditions.burn_severity_observed: sporadic, not deep — low actual severity
conditions.notes: Burn was patchy and shallow. No mineral soil exposure in most areas. Soil still hasn't warmed from winter — cold snap hasn't even hit yet (coming night of Apr 20-21).

lessons: Readiness overestimated — soil still too cold from winter
Underburn type label does not indicate adequate severity for morels
Underburn productivity is HIGHLY variable — see FR-002b for opposite case

conditions.vegetation: Mediterranean California Mesic Mixed Conifer Forest and Woodland
conditions.notes: Older RX burn (18mo). Smaller mushrooms and fewer than the younger Unit 2.3 burn nearby. Drier conditions on this side of the fire road.

lessons: 18mo RX burns DO still produce — but at reduced quantity and size
Confirms recency curve declines after first season

conditions.vegetation: Mediterranean California Mesic Mixed Conifer Forest and Woodland
conditions.notes: 5 days after the Unit 2.3 4-5lb haul. Adjacent burn unit, same age, same vegetation. Found ~2lbs but morels were smaller — possibly late-season flush, possibly foraging pressure from adjacent units. Confirms the Unit 2.x cluster as productive but suggests morels are getting smaller as season progresses in this microclimate.

lessons: Same-cluster, same-age sites can both produce — but yield diminishes over days as a flush plays out
Morels getting smaller may indicate end of primary flush
Consider tracking 'days since cluster first picked' to predict size/yield decay

conditions.vegetation: Mediterranean California Mesic Mixed Conifer Forest and Woodland
conditions.moisture: wetter (vs Waddle Ranch north of road)
conditions.notes: YOUNGER (6mo) underburn produced 4-5lbs of larger morels — significantly more than the adjacent older RX. Wetter ground south of road. Confounding: younger AND wetter, can't isolate.

lessons: Major model bug: TOO_EARLY for site with continuous GROW activity (28+ days)
extract_features only counted GROW days after a START in window — but START happened earlier
Underburn at 6mo CAN be highly productive — burn_type_score of 0.05 is too low
Model needs to recognize sites with continuous GROW even without recent START event
